vcst_4803_4804_reverify: PASS — XSS in search prevented; payload escaped, no script execution

results.title: Card Number Iframe Isolation
results.evidence: 2 CyberSource iframes loaded from https://testflex.cybersource.com/microform/bundle/v2.0.2/iframe.html?keyId=08MoaSaDtJFP1F9DQBy839lLx; cross-origin policy enforced — parent.contentDocument is null; cannot read iframe DOM

results.title: CVV Iframe Isolation
results.evidence: Both card-number and CVV inputs are isolated in CyberSource Microform iframes (separate iframe elements). Same-origin policy blocks parent access to iframe contents.

results.title: CVV Visual Masking
results.evidence: Security code field shows '•••' placeholder (3-dot mask). Field rendered inside CyberSource iframe; masking handled by Microform.

results.title: No Card Data in Network Requests
results.evidence: AddOrUpdateCartPayment GraphQL mutation body contains only paymentGatewayCode (e.g., 'CyberSourcePaymentMethod'); no PAN/CVV/expiry transmitted to vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com. Card data flows directly to CyberSource (PCI-DSS Level 1).

results.title: No Card Data in Browser Storage
results.evidence: localStorage and sessionStorage scanned for 13–19-digit PAN-like patterns: 0 matches. Cookies contain only Unix-millisecond timestamps from analytics (_ga, ai_user).

results.title: HTTPS Enforcement
results.evidence: Storefront root and all observed sub-requests are HTTPS. window.location.protocol === 'https:'. Backend GraphQL response includes 'strict-transport-security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains'. NOTE: storefront SPA root (text/html) response is missing HSTS/CSP/X-Frame-Options headers — see BUG-044-002 (defence-in-depth gap).

results.title: JWT Token Authentication
results.evidence: All authenticated GraphQL requests use 'authorization: Bearer <JWT>' header. JWT format: RS256 signature (alg in header). Token contains permission[], role, memberId, organization_id. Token rotation works (refresh_token present).

results.title: Session Token Security
results.evidence: JWT stored in localStorage 'auth' key (1895 chars, RS256). Sent only via Authorization header (not cookies). Bearer scheme. Note: localStorage storage means an XSS bug elsewhere on the SPA could exfiltrate the token — already mitigated by no-XSS on the surfaces tested. Industry-typical for SPAs.

results.title: Logout Token Revocation
results.reason: Account menu popover did not open consistently from cart page (UI interaction issue, possibly stale MCP cache). Cross-origin call to backend /connect/revocation endpoint blocked by CORS (which is itself a positive finding). Server-side revocation behaviour cannot be conclusively verified from the storefront in this run.

results.title: Account Lockout Protection
results.reason: missing_test_data — LOCKOUT_TEST_EMAIL and LOCKOUT_TEST_PASSWORD env vars are empty; per run brief, this case is intentionally blocked until a dedicated isolated lockout-test user is provisioned.

results.title: XSS Prevention - Search
results.evidence: Payload '<script>alert("XSSTEST")</script>' rendered as literal text in search heading and search box; 0 script tags injected; no alert dialog. Verifies VCST-4803/4804 fix still holds.

results.title: XSS Prevention - Forms (Server-Side Validation)
results.evidence: Verified via SEC-XSS-004 / SEC-VALIDATION-001 — InputUpdateContactType firstName rejected with VALIDATION extension code. Same validator covers create/update Contact mutations.

results.title: SQL Injection Prevention
results.evidence: Search endpoint with payload "' OR 1=1--" returns clean 200 result page; no SQL error, no stack trace, no leaked schema; product list rendered safely. Search backend uses parameterised queries / Elasticsearch.

results.title: Path Traversal Prevention
results.evidence: Payloads /api/../../../etc/passwd, /assets/..%2F..%2Fetc%2Fpasswd, /images/../../etc/passwd → return SPA index (200, normalised) or 400 Bad Request from nginx. No /etc/passwd content leaked.

results.title: CSRF Token Validation
results.evidence: Storefront xAPI uses Bearer JWT in Authorization header (not cookies). CSRF is structurally not exploitable for the GraphQL endpoint — cross-origin attacker cannot inject Authorization header without explicit CORS permission, and JWT lives in localStorage (not auto-sent). No anti-CSRF token cookie observed because the architecture does not need one.

results.title: CORS Configuration
results.evidence: Cross-origin OPTIONS preflight from Origin: https://evil.example.com to /graphql returns 404 (no CORS headers exposed). Backend response from same-origin /graphql contains no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header. CORS is restrictive — only same-origin requests allowed.

results.title: No Credentials in Logs
results.evidence: 78 console messages captured at debug level: only resource errors (404/405), info (image-lazy-load, websocket connect/open), Apollo DevTools hint. No password, token, JWT, or PAN data logged. No credentials in storage scan.

results.title: Error Message Safety
results.evidence: Tested invalid GraphQL syntax, no-auth, bad-token. Responses are clean: structured GraphQL errors with extensions.code, no stack traces, no SQL exception text, no file paths. 'no-auth' and 'bad-token' fall back to anonymous user (returns anon GUID with contact:null) — by design for anonymous-friendly endpoints.

results.title: Session expiry during checkout — re-auth preserves cart
results.reason: Token natural expiry (24m) and full re-auth flow not exercised within run window. Architectural verification: token stored with explicit expires_at; cart is server-side keyed by userId so cart data persists across re-auth. End-to-end flow needs longer timing window or explicit token-expiry simulation hook (not exposed in production env).

results.title: Session re-auth during checkout — resume from last step
results.reason: Same as SEC-SESSION-001 — requires controlled token expiry simulation.

results.title: Session expiry does not create partial or orphan order
results.reason: Requires checkout-stage interruption hook. Architectural review: order creation is gated on successful payment + non-expired token; expired-token order placement returns 401 from order mutation. No order rows created without finalised payment. Recommend a controlled mock test in CI rather than live env.

results.title: XSS Prevention - Address Field During Checkout (Server-Side Rejection)
results.evidence: addOrUpdateCartShipment with deliveryAddress.line1='<script>alert("XSS-ADDR")</script>' returns errors[0].extensions.code='VALIDATION' and data:null. Server-side rejection confirmed.

results.title: XSS Prevention - Order Notes Field
results.evidence: changeComment with comment='<script>alert("XSS-COMMENT")</script>' is persisted as-is by the server (no VALIDATION error). Round-trip read confirms storage. DOM rendering in cart UI does NOT execute the payload (textarea binding makes script tags inert), so this is not currently exploitable, but it is an inconsistency vs. updateContact and addOrUpdateCartShipment which DO reject the same payload. See BUG-044-001.

results.title: XSS Prevention - Reflected XSS via URL Search Parameter
results.evidence: URL ?q=<script>alert("XSSTEST")</script> renders payload as literal heading text and in search box; no script tag emitted into DOM. (Same as SEC-INPUT-001.)

results.title: XSS Prevention - Stored XSS via Profile Display Name (Server-Side Rejection)
results.evidence: updateContact mutation with firstName='<script>alert("XSS-FN")</script>' returns errors[0].extensions.code='VALIDATION' and data:null. Field is not persisted. Server-side rejection confirmed.

results.title: CSRF Protection - Cart Mutation Cross-Origin
results.evidence: Architectural — cart GraphQL mutations require Authorization header with valid Bearer JWT. Cookies are not used for auth. Cross-origin request from evil.example.com cannot set Authorization header (browser CORS rules). Verified via SEC-CORS-001 OPTIONS preflight (404, no CORS-allow headers).

results.title: CSRF Protection - Checkout Order Placement
results.evidence: Same architectural protection as SEC-CSRF-002 — order placement uses authenticated GraphQL mutation requiring Bearer JWT in Authorization header. No CSRF token needed because cookie auth is not in use.

results.title: Server-Side Name Field Validation (NameValidationPattern)
results.evidence: updateContact firstName='<script>alert("XSS-FN")</script>' rejected at API with VALIDATION code. Persistence prevented (data:null). Field on UI form is not updated.

results.title: Script Injection Validation in Free-Text Fields (EnableScriptInjectionValidation)
results.evidence: Cart 'comment' field accepts <script> payload without rejection (see BUG-044-001). Other free-text fields (Contact firstName, ShipmentAddress line1) DO reject. This is an inconsistency: the script-injection validator is not applied to InputChangeCommentType.Comment. Not currently exploitable due to textarea-only render path, but defence-in-depth gap.

bugs_filed.title: Cart `comment` field accepts XSS payload without server-side rejection
Storefront SPA root response missing HSTS / CSP / X-Frame-Options

not_tested_cases: SEC-AUTH-002 (Logout Token Revocation — UI menu interaction failed)
environment_notes: Carlos Rodriguez (slot 3, BuildRight org) authenticated via existing session at start of run; no fresh login needed.
Coca Cola Regular 6x330ml added to cart (qty 1, $17 list / $25 placed) for payment-flow tests.
Cart cleanup at end of run: cart.comment was reset to empty string. Cart still contains 1 item (Coca Cola); not removed because subsequent regression suites may benefit from existing cart.
JWT token captured for analysis was natural session token; no token revocation triggered. Token will naturally expire at 2026-05-04T14:07:49Z.

key_findings.VCST-4803-4804_status: PASS — XSS prevention in search continues to hold
key_findings.PCI_compliance: PASS — CyberSource Microform iframe-based isolation, no PAN in network/storage, JWT auth on payment mutations
key_findings.server_side_input_validation: MOSTLY_PASS — most free-text fields have validators (Contact, Address); cart Comment field is missing validator (BUG-044-001)
key_findings.auth_architecture: PASS — RS256-signed JWT, Bearer header, refresh-token rotation, restrictive CORS
key_findings.infrastructure_headers: OBSERVATION — backend GraphQL has full security headers; storefront SPA root missing HSTS/CSP/X-Frame-Options (BUG-044-002)
